title: Plastic waste per capita imports vs. exports
caption:
Plastic waste per capita imports vs. exports, 2024

Imports per person (tonnes; plotted on a logarithmic axis)

This scatter plot maps the relationship between plastic waste exports and imports per capita for various nations in 2024, with both axes utilizing a logarithmic scale ranging from 0.000001 to over 10 tonnes. Data points are color-coded by income group, distinguishing low-income, lower-middle-income, upper-middle-income, and high-income countries. A diagonal reference line indicates where imports equal exports; points above this line represent net importers, while those below represent net exporters. High-income countries, marked in dark green, are widely distributed but include several nations with high per capita flows. Austria, Canada, and Norway appear in the upper right quadrant, with both exports and imports exceeding 1 tonne per person. Conversely, Iceland and French Polynesia are located in the lower right, showing exports between 1 and 10 tonnes but imports below 0.001 tonnes. In the upper left, the Bahamas and Turkey show imports exceeding 1 tonne against lower export volumes. Lower-middle-income countries, shown in light purple, cluster primarily in the lower left and central regions. Nepal and Cote d'Ivoire register extremely low values on both axes, below 0.0001 tonnes. Afghanistan and Peru sit higher on the import axis, between 0.1 and 1 tonne, while maintaining lower export figures. Upper-middle-income countries like Malaysia and Azerbaijan occupy the upper middle section, with imports ranging from 1 to 10 tonnes. The visual distribution highlights a lack of symmetry, with many countries exhibiting significant disparities between the volume of plastic waste they import versus what they export per person.
